files.00_Overview.md: Add missing Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways sections at the beginning. Reorganize into 3W framework: What (incompressible flow definition, solver table), Why (convergence/accuracy benefits), How (case structure, workflow, example configs). Move detailed algorithm settings (SIMPLE/PISO/PIMPLE code blocks) to 01_Introduction.md or 03_Simulation_Control.md to reduce redundancy.
files.01_Introduction.md: Add Learning Objectives section. The 'Core Solvers' table duplicates 00_Overview.md — keep only here, remove from 00_Overview. Add 'Key Takeaways' summary section. Convert governing equations to 'What' subsection. Enhance solver selection flowchart with Mach number decision gate.
files.02_Standard_Solvers.md: Strong content — add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. The under-relaxation explanation in Concept Check Q3 duplicates 01_Introduction Q2 — consolidate. Consider adding practical example case directory structure for each solver type.
files.03_Simulation_Control.md: Good coverage of controlDict, fvSolution, fvSchemes. Add Learning Objectives. Missing fvSchemes details (currently only mentioned in overview table but not expanded). Add section on time-step estimation strategies. Add Key Takeaways summary. Troubleshooting table is good — expand with specific error messages/solutions.

global_strategy: Apply 3W Framework consistently across all files: (1) WHAT — clear definitions, solver tables, equations; (2) WHY — physical reasoning, when to use each approach; (3) HOW — code examples, step-by-step workflows. Add Learning Objectives at the top and Key Takeaways at the bottom of every file. Eliminate redundant content: solver comparison tables should appear once only (move to 01_Introduction), algorithm configuration details should live in 03_Simulation_Control not 00_Overview. All files need consistent Concept Check formatting (3 Q&A minimum). Add fvSchemes section to 03_Simulation_Control that's currently referenced but missing content.
